text.content:
Tuvstarr's Reflection

A Scenario inspired by Swedish folklore by Helge Kjellin (1913)

Safety Keywords: Decay / Corruption of body and mind / Folk Horror

Special Note: Tuvstarr's Reflection is a special Scenario built as a Scene Tree and is great for introductory Sessions, one-shots, or to introduce Sherazade the Weaver of Tales to a Campaign.

This Scenario takes place within a story told by Sherazade, an emissary of the One who Plots in the Shadows. The Hunters will awaken in the world of Princess Tuvstarr and must solve the mystery of her disappearance in order to awaken.

Good evening my dear hosts...

"Good evening my dear hosts, my name is Sherazade and I am a weaver of tales. My words will take you to incredible worlds and follow the feats of brave Hunters… however this story is not like the others. Will our heroes succeed in their quest? Or will they perish, disappearing forever from the memory of time?"

The story that led...

The Original Fairy Tale

The original fairy tale was written by Helge Kjellin in 1913 and tells how Princess Tuvstarr left her home to follow Skutt the moose into the forest.

Tuvstarr does not listen to Skutt's advice, and so the Fairies steal her crown, the Hulder (a forest spirit) steals her dress and, finally, she loses a golden heart-shaped necklace in a black pond in the depths of the woods. The Princess will stay there staring at the black pond until she turns into a blade of grass: Tuvstarr (Carex Cespitosa).

But Stories Don't Always Go as Originally Written...

Appalled by the wickedness of the world, Princess Tuvstarr learns from rumors around the court of the existence of a pond, deep in the woods, where wishes can come true. Tuvstarr asks the trusty moose Skutt to lead her through the woods. There, the Princess trades her crown with the Fairies to gain free access to their lands.

Tuvstarr then gives the Hulder her dress to continue her search, and finally arrives at the heart of the forest. There, in the black Pond that no human being has ever seen, she plunges her heart and soul. Tuvstarr desires a perfect world, where everything is bright and good. And that's what she finds on the other side. A perfect dream world. There she is happy, the fields are always green, and idealized constructs that resemble her mother and father smile at her.

But the Dream World needs to balance itself out. And so reality, its mirror, becomes dark and twisted. The sun sets in eternal darkness, the forest is corrupted, animals get sick, and the soil rots.

pages.name: The Story at the Arrival of the Hunters

text.content:
The story at the arrival of the Hunters...

The land is bewitched. The fields are sterile, the church bells are silent, mothers forget to feed their children, and all dreams are dark and full of pain. The sun now rests below the horizon and the lands are covered by eternal twilight.

Tuvstarr the Child Princess is gone, wandering into the forest and the curse is believed to have something to do with her disappearance. The knights were sent to the forest, the wetlands, and the wilderness to find her, but none have returned. The King and Queen now seek out new champions to retrieve their daughter from the wilds and end this curse.

Tuvstarr's father and mother, the King and Queen, send their knights to bring her back. None of them returns. The lands are becoming increasingly corrupt and the people are desperate. Eventually, the rulers turn to anyone who can help them. This is where our story begins.

Main NPCs

Princess Tuvstarr

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a pure and innocent soul, who just wants a world without suffering.

Agenda: I want to stay forever in my perfect world. I have been here for so long that I have forgotten what my life was like before.

Gift – Creative Will: Tuvstarr has the power to create anything she wants in the Dream World. She can make a bird appear out of nowhere or make an apple tree grow from nothing to bearing fruit in a couple of seconds. She can deflect a river, make sunlight warmer, shape clouds, and make small fish glow around her when swimming in a river. Each new creation draws life from the real world, making it more corrupt and darker. Tuvstarr can use her power to defend herself by literally manipulating the whole world. If Tuvstarr is killed in this world, she will transform into colorful butterflies to regenerate herself at her castle. Although Tuvstarr has virtually infinite power, her innocence means that even if threatened, she will never try to kill a living being. Rather, the Princess will try to hurt and scare any threats and, in the worst case, drive them away.

Gift – Pure Innocence: Tuvstarr radiates innocence and beauty. Whoever meets her will fall under her spell. She is always friendly, and her voice is clear and beautiful, with the sharpness of ice breaking on a winter pond. Those who interact with Tuvstarr must make a Defense Check or be fascinated by her. For the rest of the Scene, the bewitched subject will not take any action that could harm Tuvstarr and, furthermore, will defend her at the cost of their life from any threats. Seeing how the world outside the pond is rotting will make a bewitched subject instantly come to their senses.

Skutt the Moose (Corrupted - Real World)

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a majestic and magnificent moose, ravaged by the decay of death and Tuvstarr's magic which keeps me alive.

Agenda: I have to find someone to save Tuvstarr from the black pond and the illusion she fell into, because I can't get in / Protect Tuvstarr above all else, even if it means having the world rot away.

Gift - Spectral Presence: Skutt cannot truly be eliminated as long as Tuvstarr's wish keeps him alive. The moose can move through fog, disappearing and reappearing even many feet away. Within the fog, no one can stop or impede Skutt's movement. When he suffers his third Wound, Skutt falls apart, only to revive in the following Scene.

Background: He was once a majestic being, the King of the Woods and Wildlands. The name Skutt was given to him by the rocks, torrents, and small streams, as he had the ability to make great leaps through any obstacle. One day, when the sun was bright, he ventured into the fields and flowers near the castle that humans built. There, he met Tuvstarr and was fascinated by the little creature with her white dress, long golden hair and sparkling crown. When Tuvstarr asked him to see the rest of the world, he couldn't refuse and put her on his back to ride into the wilderness. He told her about the pond in the middle of the woods where all wishes can be fulfilled, but only if the wish is made by an innocent child. And so she asked to go there. During the journey the Princess traded both the crown and the dress for safe passage, and at the pond she disappeared into the black water, leaving only her reflection. Then there was a crack in the sky, which froze the sun in place forever. Not knowing what to do, Skutt wandered the lands in shame. He came across one of the knights sent by the King and Queen. The noble warrior charged the moose with his spear couched under his arm. The moose faced him head-on and killed the armored foe and his steed. But the spear had hit its mark and Skutt fell, dying. However, the power of Tuvstarr's affection would not allow him to die. The Princess was so anxious that she wished that Skutt could exist forever, even in death. The moose is now rotting. Large worms crawl into his flesh, and in the moonlight, they hatch into ghostly moths that flutter around his horns. His stench is carried by the wind and even carrion-eating birds avoid him. At the time of his death, he forgot his name. But he can't forget Tuvstarr and the guilt he feels...

Encounter Information: Skutt is a Main NPC that Hunters can encounter in any Scene. He will always appear accompanied by a thick fog, stinking of rot, and surrounded by pale moths. From his horns, like a ghostly veil, hangs the torn fabric of a knightly cloak, and a spear is half driven into his body.

  • If the Hunters fight: Skutt is not interested in the fight but is still a proud animal, so he will lash out at the enemy, charging with his antlers.
  • If the Hunters talk to Skutt: He can tell how he took Tuvstarr to the sacred pond, deep in the woods. Whatever Tuvstarr did by diving into the lake, it wreaked havoc on the world. Skutt wants to save Tuvstarr, but without endangering her. He cannot enter the pond himself, nor can any other inhabitant of these woods. The magic of the pond binds anyone seeking it to make the same journey as the Princess, crossing the places where she exchanged her treasures to continue forward. If the Hunters bring those treasures to him, Skutt will lead them to the pond where Tuvstarr has disappeared.

Skutt the Moose (Dream Version)

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a majestic and magnificent moose who protects the sweet Princess Tuvstarr with all his might.

Agenda: Protect Tuvstarr from all threats and make her happy / Listen to a small part of my soul that cries out that this perfect world is just a wishful illusion.

Note: Skutt is always near Tuvstarr in the Dream World and will appear as soon as the Hunters encounter the Princess. For the Hunters it will be clear that this is not the real Skutt, but another dream created by Tuvstarr. Skutt follows the Princess and she rides him through the kingdom. Since he is a dream, this Skutt will have no recollection of the Hunters. In reality, a spark of the original Skutt resides in his dream projection and can be exploited by the Hunters to push him to remember the real world.

The Gallant Knight

OL Medium 5 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am a respected and experienced knight of the kingdom. My skill is only matched by my honor.

Agenda: Respect my knightly duties and serve the young Princess Tuvstarr / Save the kingdom from what's happening.

Gift - Gallant Knight: The Knight is incredibly skilled, but he would never use his full abilities against anyone he doesn't consider truly evil. When defending against an enemy attack, his mastery increases his OL by 1. The Knight will never inflict the final Wound on a target if he doesn't feel it truly deserves death.

Encounter: The Gallant Knight sits under a tree in the Dream World, eating an apple. His sword is beside him. He is an old man, but there is still strength in his steps and voice. In fact, his hair isn't as grey as it used to be. His pains have lessened. An old wound that once grieved him is now merely a white scar. The Knight has abandoned his quest. He found his way to the black pond, entered the deep waters, and arrived in the Dream World. He met Tuvstarr, tried to bring her back, but she offered to let him stay here. He agreed, vowing to continue to protect her from any threat. However, he can be convinced to pick up his quest once more if he is told that his brothers all died in the search and if he is shown the two-tailed fox crest. If the Hunters possess the Lance of the Fallen Knight, the Gallant Knight will ask them to return it to him so that it can be brought back to the castle in the real world. Whether defeated or convinced, the Knight will reveal that Tuvstarr is in the Dream World.

Threats and Creatures

Fairies

OL Easy 3 – Minor NPC (1 Wound)

Descriptor: We are Fairies, spirits of nature who move gracefully in a world of clumsy humans.

Agenda: Defend the crown that Tuvstarr has given us and dance forever wrapped in its sweet light.

Gift – Madness: Made mad by the light of the crown, the Fairies rage at anyone who tries to touch it. Two or more Fairies can attack together to make a single action with a bonus to the OL equal to the number of fairies involved. If the action succeeds, in addition to inflicting 1 Wound, the target will be thrown into the swamp to be drowned. Once in the water, the target can attempt to break out of the flurry of drowned, dead hands pulling from the bottom with a Defense Check against OL Medium 5.

The Hulder - Corrupted Forest Spirit

OL Hard 7 – Main NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an ancient forest spirit. I have eaten so many living things that I have become fat and swollen, and the pieces of the animals and people I have devoured are dripping from my slobbering lips.

Agenda: Devour everything I meet.

Gift – Corrupted Spirit: The Hulder has a feminine form, thin but her skin is spongy and slimy. Her breath smells of rot. She doesn't realize what a grotesque monster she has become, as in her mind she is still beautiful and seductive. When she suffers 1 Wound, the Hulder vomits a cloud of foul vapor that reeks of rot. All the subjects hit by the cloud suffer 1 Wound in turn. The effect of this Gift can be avoided with a Defense Check.

Gift – Swollen with Flesh and Blood: If killed, the Hulder will emit a long, heart-breaking cry that will echo in the woods, and all the dead she has eaten will scream in agony in a cacophonous chorus. Anyone who hears the cry loses 2 Soma and receives 1 Penalty on any action for the rest of the Scene.

Background: This is the Hulder's realm. A spiritual forest entity with feminine traits that once lived in symbiosis with trees but is now corrupt and repulsive. Tuvstarr traded her dress for passage through the woods. The Hulder took the thin fabric in her hands, and it immediately made her feel unlike anything she had felt before. With the dress, she felt strong and beautiful, but also prey to a voracious hunger. Now the dress is nothing more than a dirty piece of cloth that the spirit has tied over her eyes like a blindfold. The Hulder obsessively repeats how beautiful, graceful and lovely she is. This can be a hint to help the Hunters realize that with the blindfold on the monster is unaware of her true appearance.

Defeating the Hulder: It is easy to spot the tracks of the Hulder. A trail of blood and slime is left on the ground where the creature crawls. Her sense of smell is almost non-existent, but if the Hunters make a noise entering her part of the forest, she will charge furiously forward to devour them. The Hulder is powerful and killing her in a fight will be difficult. The best way to defeat her is to lure her into a trap so as to tear the dirty piece of cloth – the dress Tuvstarr gave her – from her eyes. If this happens near a reflective surface (such as one of the many ponds that fill this area), she will see her reflection and what she has become. She will scream in agony and crawl away into the ashes of the night. There, she will throw up everything she ate before returning to her original form, filled with shame and guilt.

Stallo the Troll

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (3 Wounds)

Descriptor: I am an old troll and my appetite is matched only by my strength. A bird has made its nest on my head and when it gets angry it starts to peck me in the eye, which is incredibly annoying!

Gift – Troll: Stallo possesses enormous strength and each Position Check for a physical action attempted against him receives 1 Penalty. At the end of each of his actions, Stallo recovers 1 Wound he suffered. When he reaches 0 Wounds, the troll will collapse to the ground, only to regenerate within minutes.

Angry Little Girl

OL Medium 5 – Minor NPC (2 Wounds)

Descriptor: I survived the adults' madness and now I struggle like a ferocious animal not to succumb to this cruel world.

Agenda: Find a safe place to stay or a group of people ready to accept and protect me.

Key Locations

The Royal Castle

Descriptor: In the midst of a field of withered flowers and rotting crops stands a mighty castle. The walls are covered with ivy and the roofs, once gilded, seem blackened and burned by the rays of a dying, red and menacing sun.

Information: The royal castle is in the center of a large city. The air is gloomy, and the faces of the people are sad and dejected. A black sky illuminated by a faint crimson light hangs over everything like an eternal night. It is early morning on a gloomy autumn day.

The Wild Forest

Descriptor: The woods before you are shrouded in perpetual darkness. Only the faintest light of the sun hanging just beyond the horizon reaches this place. But the sun never really rises, leaving this land enclosed in a twilight and ghostly hour. The creatures of the woods have gone mad. Corrupted by the whispers in the grass, mesmerized by the moon's reflections, they drink poisonous sap and foam that smells of sulphur.

Information: The wood that separates the kingdom from the deepest forest is basked in the faintest twilight glow. Time has stopped and no longer has any meaning. The closer you get to the wilderness and the great forest, the more the atmosphere changes.

The Swamp of the Fairies

Descriptor: The swamps are shrouded in thin veils of a ghostly mist. All is silent. The moon appears to crack and bleed into the sky, and the faint stars die out one by one as you travel deeper into this nocturnal realm. There is a light in the distance, a glow that is reflected in the swamps and still water. This light comes from a small golden crown, and all around, many silhouettes dance as if enthralled by its light.

Threat - Marshy Swamp (OL Medium 5): A stone track provides the only path. The water is black, cold, and deep enough to slow a Hunter who falls into it.

Information: The crown is sitting on a small islet in an open swamp. It is possible to reach it by jumping between the various stones that emerge from the water. Not far away, sitting in the damp mud, are the bodies of all those who have drowned here over the centuries. They have emerged from the earth, awakened from the dead, to watch the Fairies dance. They sit, motionless, observing the show with dull eyes and empty eye sockets.

The Dark Forest (Hulder's Realm)

Descriptor: This area of the forest is even darker than any you have been through so far. The woods are deep, dark, and full of evil shadows. You come across giant boulders entirely covered in dense moss and bearded lichens hanging from tree branches, with the cold wind whipping through the foliage. On the ground you see a streak of blood and slime, as if forming a gloomy path that guides your steps.

Information: Among the trees of a dark forest, where not even the light of the moon dares to show itself. It is night. Darkness envelops the forest, giving the trees ghostly shapes.

The Black Pond

Descriptor: You arrive at the center of the forest. The trees are tall and menacing, and they seem clustered around a pool of freezing, black water. Any light that penetrates here looks thin and weak, as if it were an intruder in this land consecrated to darkness. Everything is still and silent.

Information: The center of the great forest, bathed in an unnatural darkness. An indefinite hour, in a place out of the normal flow of time. As Hunters approach the water, they can discover two things: 1) While the princess is gone, Tuvstarr's reflection can still be seen in the black waters, showing an image of a naked girl sitting on the shore and staring into the abyss. Next to her is a knight's plate armor. The shield has a fox with two tails as a heraldic symbol. There are footsteps on the muddy shore leading into the black water. 2) To enter the mirror world, the Hunters will have to dive and swim to the bottom of the Pond, through the cold and fatal darkness. When they are at the bottom, in the mud and slime, suddenly a light will appear from above. Sunlight in a clear sky. By swimming to the surface, the Hunters will enter the Dream World that Tuvstarr has built for herself.

The Dream World

Descriptor: It is a land of joy, happiness, and goodness. Here the days are eternal, bees never sting, and nature provides everything those who live there need. It is built on all that is good, siphoned out of the real world and remodelled according to the will of the Child Princess. The grass is emerald green, the sky is blue with perfect white clouds. A thousand and a thousand more flowers. Butterflies and pollen in the air. Everything is lush and new. What was once a quagmire is a field with rolling hills, what used to be a forbidding forest is now a lush wood with flourishing trees and meadows carpeted with flowers. In the distance, the high towers of the castle reflect the sunlight.

Information: Early morning, on a beautiful summer day. When they arrive, the Hunters will feel like they have been reborn. Their wounds will stop hurting (it is considered a special rest that allows all Hunters to recover 1 Wound). Their garments and clothes will be mended, in fact looking better than ever before. The water they drink and the fruit they eat are the best meal they have ever eaten. It is truly a paradise. However, everything seems to be nothing more than a false reflection of reality. Farmers at work, animals in the fields, the King and Queen themselves at the gates of the castle, all do nothing but smile and nod. They speak in a low voice and say comforting things like: "Stay here, everything is fine, the lands are fresh and full of life." They are nothing more than simple, idealized creations of Tuvstarr's memories, but full of joy and warmth.

pages.name: Random Encounters in the Wild Forest

text.content:
Random Encounters in the Wild Forest

When crossing the wood, the Hunters may have some encounters. Roll a die or choose:

  1. A fox is killing and eating its own pups while giving birth to them.
  2. Hugin and Munin: Two ravens, Hugin and Munin, messengers of Father Odin, approach the Hunters. Even the ancient gods want to stop this disease. If a Hunter approaches the ravens, they will realize they can mentally communicate with them. The ravens want to follow the Hunters. Anyone who makes a pact with them receives the Hugin and Munin Scenario Gift, without the need to have an Interlude.
  3. Lance of the Fallen Knight: Black fumes fill the air, foamy and dense bubbles emerging from a dark pond. A knight in rusty armor lies dead near one of the springs. His eyes are white and puffy, like a sack of eggs ready to burst. The corpse holds an ashwood spear with an iron point. If a Hunter chooses to take the spear, he gains the Lance of the Fallen Knight Scenario Gift without the need to have an Interlude.
  4. Stallo the Troll: The Troll Stallo sleeps sitting under a tree, and a bird has built a nest in his hair. If he is awakened by too much noise or for any other reason, he will try to eat the Hunters.
  5. The White Warhorse: A saddled warhorse, magnificent and white as fresh snow, is grazing in the tall grass. This mount belonged to one of the knights who met a dire fate in the search for Tuvstarr. The steed will be reluctant to be approached, but if the Hunters succeed (Threat OL Medium 5, Descriptor: I am a fast, mighty, and frightened horse), it will take them to the body of its old master, where the Hunters can retrieve the knightly crest showing a fox with two tails. This crest will be useful when meeting the Gallant Knight.
  6. Ruined Castle: A ruined castle on a hill. The castle is the scene of a massacre. The men and women who once lived within its walls have apparently killed each other. Only a ten-year-old girl remains alive, but she is angry and will try to attack the Hunters with a hidden knife.

Exclusive Scenario Gifts

These Gifts represent special abilities related to the Tuvstarr's Reflection Scenario and can be acquired once the requirements are met. A Hunter can only have one Scenario Gift and if they acquire a new one, they will have to forgo the previous one. Unlike with the normal rules, in this case an Interlude is not necessary to acquire the Gifts.

Hugin and Munin

Requirement: To meet Hugin and Munin and make a pact with them.

Once per Scene, you can ask for advice from Hugin and Munin. Ask the Storyteller a question, choosing from among: "Where should I go?" "How can I defeat that enemy?" "What is missing in my search?" The Storyteller will answer with a cryptic sentence, which always includes a useful hint. Get 1 Additional Success on all Defense Checks against an attack from a non-human creature, thanks to the blessing of Odin himself. At the beginning of each Scene, you must spend 1 Soma to feed the spirits of Hugin and Munin, or lose the use of this Gift for the rest of the Scenario.

Lance of the Fallen Knight

Requirement: Collect the lance of the knight who died in the search for Tuvstarr.

When you wield this spear and obtain an Outcome with Increment on an attack, you can inflict 1 Additional Wound. The tip of the lance can ignite at will, casting a light and allowing you to have a burning fire always near at hand.

The Scenario in Scenes

Tuvstarr's Reflection is built as a Scene Tree with branching paths.

FIRST SCENE – The Royal Castle: The Hunters awaken in a strange inn and are summoned by the King and Queen. An old butler offers gold and honors to retrieve Princess Tuvstarr from the great forest. Before departure, the butler warns that extreme measures may be necessary—perhaps even killing the Princess herself. Villagers report seeing Tuvstarr ride a moose into the woods, after which the world changed.

SECOND SCENE – The Wild Forest: Traveling through the corrupted wilderness, the Hunters may encounter various threats and opportunities (fox eating its young, Hugin and Munin ravens, fallen knight's lance, Stallo the troll, white warhorse, ruined castle). Skutt the rotting moose may appear in thick fog at any time.

THIRD SCENE OPTIONS: The Hunters must recover both Tuvstarr's crown and dress to progress.

  • OPTION A – The Swamp of the Fairies: Navigate the treacherous swamp to retrieve Tuvstarr's golden crown from dancing Fairies driven mad by its light. The crown sits on an islet surrounded by black water and the drowned dead.
  • OPTION B – The Hulder's Forest: Track the corrupted forest spirit who wears Tuvstarr's dress as a blindfold. Defeat her by removing the blindfold near a reflective surface so she sees what she's become.

FOURTH SCENE – The Black Pond: At the heart of the forest, dive through the freezing black water to reach the bottom. A light appears from above—sunlight. Swimming up brings the Hunters to Tuvstarr's Dream World, a paradise of eternal summer where all wounds heal and everything is perfect.

FIFTH SCENE – The Castle of Dreams: In the Dream World, encounter the Gallant Knight who abandoned his quest to stay in paradise. Find Princess Tuvstarr wandering her perfect kingdom. She will ask the Hunters to stay forever. Convince her to leave by showing her the crown and dress, speaking of Skutt's suffering, or with the Gallant Knight's help. Fighting Tuvstarr is extremely difficult—she can reshape reality itself and regenerates at her castle. The only way to free everyone is to bring Tuvstarr back through the black pond, ending her wish and restoring balance to the world.

ENDING: Returning Tuvstarr dissolves the Dream World. The sun returns to the lands, animals recover, and Skutt can finally rest. Tuvstarr returns to her parents filled with sadness, facing an uncertain future. The Hunters awaken from Sherazade's story, forever changed by the tragic tale.

Confronting Princess Tuvstarr

The Hunters may encounter Tuvstarr in the village, the forest, or the castle. She is simply a child who wanders, naked and full of life. Tuvstarr goes around creating things in her dream world.

Tuvstarr's Request

Tuvstarr will ask the Hunters to remain in her kingdom, just like the Gallant Knight. If they agree, the Hunters will spend their lives in a dream and will soon forget about the world on the other side of the pond.

Fighting Tuvstarr

Fighting Tuvstarr is a very difficult challenge. But if the Hunters manage to imprison her or push her near the lake, they may throw her in to make her emerge on the other side.

Convincing Tuvstarr to Leave

Getting her to leave her dream is difficult as she only has vague memories of the real world, as if that were the dream to her. She does not have the ability to "remove" the black pond with her power, as it is the gateway between worlds. Several approaches may work:

  • Skutt's Suffering: Talking about Skutt's painful existence will confuse her and could be a lever to convince her to follow the Hunters' advice. The black pond scares her like the pond in the forest, but she cannot remove it with her Creative Will.
  • The Gallant Knight: If persuaded back to his mission, the Knight could help convince Tuvstarr to trust the Hunters.
  • The Crown and the Dress: The Hunters will be able to show Tuvstarr the crown that enchanted the fairies and the dress that she gave to Hulder in order to make her recall memories of the real world. The Princess will have a vague memory of owning and trading them. Taking them in her hand, she will recognize them. She will remember the weight of the crown on her head and the warmth of the dress's fabric against her fingers. Wearing her items, she will realize the difference between what is dream and what is reality, and will begin to cry. At this point, however, she will be even more strongly opposed to the idea of returning to the real world.

The Dream Skutt

Skutt is always near Tuvstarr and will appear as soon as the Hunters encounter the Princess. For the Hunters it will be clear that this is not the real Skutt, but another dream created by Tuvstarr. Skutt follows the Princess and she rides him through the kingdom. Since he is a dream, this Skutt will have no recollection of the Hunters. In reality, a spark of the original Skutt resides in his dream projection and can be exploited by the Hunters to push him to remember the real world.

A Sad Ending

"You take Tuvstarr back across the pond as her Dream World dissolves. The sun returns to the lands. The madness that has infested the animals is forgotten as a passing nightmare. Skutt can finally let go, sighing in relief as it is time to lay down his big head and forgive Tuvstarr for her transgressions. Tuvstarr, after mourning the death of her dear friend, returns to her parents' arms filled with sadness. An uncertain future awaits her. Perhaps she will become a wise queen or maybe they will find her little body floating in a river, drowned as she tries to return to a Dream World that no longer exists."

"We have come to the end of this story. My thanks to all those who have had the patience to listen to the words of this humble storyteller. But even the most enchanting places cannot delight when you are there against your will, and at this point I think it is time to say goodbye to our protagonists, knowing that the tragic story of Princess Tuvstarr has also touched their hearts. But let the sadness at our parting be tempered by anticipation, for our heroes will be caught in another story before too long."

"With gratitude, Sherazade the Weaver of Tales."

flags.setting: A cursed kingdom trapped in eternal twilight, the corrupted wild forest, the Dream World
specialMechanics: Quest for treasures to unlock locations
